explanation: The requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' shall also hold in that situation. This is a safety property ensuring that 'prop_1' cannot be true without 'prop_0' also being true. In LTL, this is usually formalized as G (prop_1 -> prop_0).
The requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must also hold. In LTL, this is expressed globally: whenever 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' must also be true at the same time.

This requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' holds (is true), 'prop_0' shall also hold. In LTL, we express this as 'globally (if prop_1 then prop_0)', i.e., whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at that time.

This sentence is ambiguous, but it can be interpreted as prop_0 occurring at the same time as prop_1 holds, or whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 also holds. Therefore, globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must be true as well.

This is interpreted as: whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 should also hold at that time. In other words, at every moment in time, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true. This is a standard temporal pattern for 'whenever' or 'when'.

The requirement states that whenever prop_1 holds, then prop_0 shall hold at the same time or immediately after. In LTL, to capture this causality, we use G (globally): always, whenever prop_1 happens, prop_0 must also happen at the same instant.
Whenever 'prop_1' occurs, 'prop_0' must happen at the same time or afterwards. In LTL, this can be represented as: globally, if 'prop_1' occurs, then eventually 'prop_0' must occur.

This requirement says that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must also hold at the same time. In LTL, this is a response property which is best represented as: globally, if 'prop_1' holds, then 'prop_0' must hold.

This specification requires that whenever prop_1 becomes true, prop_0 must also become true (either immediately or at some point thereafter). In LTL, a common way to capture this is to require that always (G), if prop_1 holds, then eventually (F) prop_0 will hold.
Whenever 'prop_1' becomes true, the autopilot must ensure that 'prop_0' also holds at that moment. In LTL, this is typically written as 'globally, if prop_1 then prop_0'.
